methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
CubeSpace is a growth-stage company in Space & Aerospace, founded by Mike-Alec Kearney. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, reflecting a 13-company comp set across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods are inapplicable here. Peer-Median benchmarking fails due to insufficient narrow peer cohorts: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering for matching stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier yields fewer than the required 3 datapoints. The Peer-Median approach was rejected rather than widened to all-sector medians, which would introduce material distortion. Public-Comp anchoring cannot proceed because CubeSpace's revenue is not on file—standard for private companies—and applying the Space & Aerospace P/S median of 53.2x across 6 public tickers would rest on an absent input.

Without executable quantitative methods, this analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range or point estimate. The primary constraint is data asymmetry: public sector multiples (53.2x P/S) exist but lack a private revenue denominator for application. Key uncertainties blocking further analysis include CubeSpace's revenue, profitability profile, and growth trajectory relative to the identified private peer set. Any valuation work would require disclosure of financial metrics or completion of a detailed bottom-up build from disclosed funding rounds and operational milestones.
